item.title: Musk’s xAI Promised Staff $420 for Their Tax Returns, Hasn’t Paid
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI asked employees earlier this year to offer up their own tax returns as training data for the company’s Grok chatbot, promising a $420 payment as incentive for doing so. Two months later, those payments still haven’t materialized.

item.title: Anthropic’s Co-Founder to Launch Encyclical on AI With Pope Leo
item.description: Pope Leo XIV’s launch of his first encyclical focusing on artificial intelligence next week will feature an unexpected guest: Christopher Olah, co-founder of Anthropic.

item.title: Citi, BlackRock’s HPS Sign €15 Billion Private Credit Agreement
item.description: Citigroup Inc. and BlackRock Inc.’s private credit unit HPS Investment Partners have struck an agreement to collaborate on direct-lending deals across Europe, targeting as much as €15 billion ($17.5 billion) of financings over the next five years.

item.title: Bitcoin Depot Goes Bankrupt, Signaling Demise of Crypto ATMs
item.description: Bitcoin Depot Inc., once the largest operator of crypto ATMs in North America, filed for bankruptcy on Monday, marking the latest major blow for a niche segment of the digital asset industry that has been in decline for several years.

item.title: Belarus Starts Nuclear Drills With Russia After Kyiv’s Warnings
item.description: Belarus announced that its armed forces would conduct snap nuclear exercises alongside Russian allies, following warnings from Kyiv that it could retaliate if Minsk becomes more deeply involved in the Kremlin’s war.

item.title: India Tells State Banks to Use EVs, Video Calls to Cut Fuel Use
item.description: India’s state-run banks have been directed by the government to use video conferencing facilities for meetings and limit overseas travel to reduce fuel usage and preserve foreign exchange reserves.

item.title: Merck Starts Seven-Part High-Grade Bond Sale to Fund Terns Deal
item.description: Merck & Co. has kicked off an investment-grade bond sale to help finance its $6.7 billion acquisition of Terns Pharmaceuticals Inc.

item.title: Chile’s Economy Contracts Despite Optimism Over Kast Government
item.description: Chile’s economy contracted in the first quarter even as market-friendly proposals from the nation’s new President Jose Antonio Kast stoked investor optimism.

item.title: Google’s Own AI Researchers Jockey for Access to Its Computing
item.description: In the race to build the infrastructure that powers artificial intelligence, Alphabet Inc.’s Google has an enviable position: The company has a healthy cloud computing business, makes its own chips, and has struck deals to share them with companies like Anthropic PBC and Meta Platforms Inc.

item.title: Poland Delays Phase Out of €600 Billion Loan Benchmark to 2036
item.description: Poland has delayed the phase out of the Wibor interbank benchmark for existing loan agreements until end 2036 following criticism for the plan from commercial lenders wary of a switch to a new index.

item.title: US Futures Waver as Oil Prices Edge Lower, Chips Stocks Rally
item.description: US futures swung to slight gains in Monday pre-market trading, erasing overnight losses as oil prices retreated from an earlier surge, and as top semiconductor stocks including Micron Technology Inc. and Nvidia Corp. advanced. Sentiment remained muted, though, with inflation remaining a key concern.

item.title: Inflation Uptick Is Starting to Send Sell Signals to Stock Bulls
item.description: Wall Street strategists are warning the honeymoon period for stocks following a blockbuster earnings season is over and that a harsh macro-economic reality now threatens this year’s rally.

item.title: Chile Mortgage Rates Hit Four-Year Low, Easing Industry Crisis
item.description: Government subsidies have pushed Chile’s average mortgage rate below 4% for the first time in more than four years, boosting sales of cheaper homes, while failing to trigger a broader revival in the construction industry.

item.title: Brazil Analysts Raise 2026 Key Rate Bets on Price Pressures
item.description: Brazil economists raised their key rate forecast for the end of this year while also boosting their 2026 inflation estimates for the 10th straight week as domestic demand and the Iran war drive price pressures.

item.title: Czech Central Bank Chief Ready to Hike If Core Price Risks Grow
item.description: Czech central bank Governor Ales Michl said policymakers are ready to raise interest rates if global and domestic risks threaten to boost underlying inflation pressures.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 18, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.4% as of 7:37 a.m. in New York. Investors are watching bond markets to see if Friday’s surge in yields to multi-year highs would extend.

item.title: NextEra to Buy Dominion for $67 Billion to Form Power Giant
item.description: NextEra Energy Inc. agreed to pay about $67 billion in stock for Dominion Energy Inc. in the biggest power acquisition ever, creating a giant utility extending from Florida to the data centers clustered in Virginia.

item.title: Nuclear Startup Submits Plan for Six Small Swedish Reactors
item.description: Swedish nuclear startup Blykalla AB has submitted an application to the government to build six small reactors to help meet the anticipated growth in power demand in the decades ahead.

item.title: European Markets Watchdog Shortlists Six Officials for Top Job
item.description: The European Securities and Markets Authority has drawn up a shortlist of six candidates to lead the watchdog that’s set to be handed beefed-up powers.

item.title: Commodity Merchant BGN Adds More Senior Oil Traders to Its Ranks
item.description: BGN added two more senior oil traders to its roster, the latest in a series of industry veterans to join the commodity merchant.

item.title: Russia’s Flagship Gas Pipeline to China Remains in Xi’s Hands
item.description: A pipeline deal will be on the agenda when Russia’s president visits Beijing this week.

item.title: UK to Scrap Parts of Ring-Fencing Rules to Boost Lending
item.description: The UK government revealed plans to scrap some of the ring-fencing regime that forces the separation of large banks’ retail and trading arms, a move that Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said would unlock extra financing for British firms.

item.title: Markets Are Undervaluing Video Game Talent and IP, Investor Says
item.description: Kensei Capital founder wants to help game makers scale up

item.title: Bond Jitters Test G-7 Finance Chiefs’ Nerves in Paris
item.description: An alarming debt-market selloff is intruding on a meeting of finance ministers.

item.title: Trump-Xi ‘Placeholder’ Summit Leaves Room for Tensions to Reignite
item.description: The meeting was long on good vibes. The question now is, how long will they last?

item.title: SpaceX’s Mega-IPO Puts a Price Tag on the Fear of Missing Out
item.description: Elon Musk has turned science fiction into reality for the better part of two decades, making believers rich and earning the benefit of the doubt from many on Wall Street. Away from rockets and electric cars, his companies are bending reality on another plane – convincing analysts to stretch their traditional valuation tools, and assign multiples based on what one academic calls “optionalities.”

item.title: IMF Tells UK to Stick to Fiscal Plan as Starmer Rivals Circle
item.description: The International Monetary Fund urged the UK to stick to plans to reduce borrowing and warned of limited space for tax increases as investors brace for a challenge to Keir Starmer’s premiership that could signal changes in economic policy.

item.title: Reasons Why Investors Are Selling Government Bonds From UK to US
item.description: Bonds are buckling around the world, propelling borrowing costs to multi-year highs as concerns over war-driven inflation and government spending mount.

item.title: Yield Surge Puts Logic Behind Stocks Rally to the Test
item.description: Equities are showing signs of resilience even as yields jump.

item.title: EU Plans Tougher Trade Measures to Rebuff Chinese Export Surge
item.description: European Union officials are exploring whether the bloc needs new measures to protect its economy against an influx of Chinese goods, according to people familiar with the matter.
